pi_items.source: Confirmed via PHP injection in setup_task.sh and SQL query in export_result.sh.
Confirmed via task description and strict equality check in verifier.py.
Confirmed via verifier.py which checks if priority is "P1" (SuiteCRM"s backend value for "High").
Confirmed via task description and SQL query in export_result.sh.
Confirmed via PHP injection in setup_task.sh.
Confirmed via file generation in setup_task.sh and check in verifier.py.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated CRM records and a synthetic Apache log file created during setup. The target case name ("Web Server Random Crashes - TechCorp") and contact name ("Alice Smith") are explicitly injected into the SuiteCRM database via PHP. The expected case status ("Assigned") and priority ("P1", corresponding to "High" in the UI) are enforced by the verifier script. The required note subject ("Client Apache Error Log") and attachment filename ("Apache_2k.log") match the task instructions, setup file generation, and verification queries.
